programme.title: Top 10: Secrets and Mysteries: SECRETS AND MAGIC OF ALTERNATIVE MEDICINE
programme.desc: In this episode, we dive into untraditional healing and its mysteries, challenging conventional Western medicine. Explore alternative medical miracles that will captivate you.

programme.title: Top 10: Secrets and Mysteries: SACRED BUILDINGS
programme.desc: Top 10 Secrets and Mysteries looks at the sacred places and buildings of ancient and medieval people including the Great Pyramid of Giza, Chartres Cathedral, Derinkuyu Underground City, Santiago de Compostela Catherdral.

programme.title: Top 10: Secrets and Mysteries: MYSTERIES OF HIDDEN ROOMS
programme.desc: Throughout history, humanity has been captivated by hidden places—rooms, passages, and chambers. Whether for escape or sinister motives, these ten notable hidden spaces span the ages, revealing intrigue and mystery.

programme.title: Myth Hunters
programme.desc: Ron Wyatt, a Nashville nurse turned amateur archaeologist at 47, aimed to validate Bible stories. In 1977, he claimed finding Noah’s Ark on Mount Ararat and later dived for Egyptian chariot remains, supporting Moses’ Red Sea crossing.

programme.title: Myth Hunters
programme.desc: California Gold Rush prospector goes in search of a legendary lost treasure ship in the Mojave Desert.

programme.title: Myth Hunters
programme.desc: According to legend, King Arthur was Britain’s greatest king. He led his Knights of the Round Table from Camelot, a spectacular castle, and fought Anglo-Saxon invaders, uniting the country. Leslie Alcock led a team to find Camelot.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: In 2006, Nina Sharanova vanished in Oakland. Her ex-husband, Hans Reiser, was convicted of her murder after their son’s testimony and leading police to her body. Reiser is now appealing the conviction.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: In 1990, Danny Rolling killed five students in Gainesville, Florida. He confessed to the crimes, pleaded guilty to avoid the death penalty, but was ultimately sentenced to death and executed 16 years later.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: In a gruesome 1989 case, Tracey Wigginton and her accomplices lure Edward Baldock with promises of a good time, only to brutally stab him to death, sparking a sensational trial that raised questions about the roles of her accomplices.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: In 2004, Marc Dutroux was sentenced to life for kidnapping, raping, and murdering young girls in Belgium. His 1996 arrest and delayed trial exposed horrific crimes and official failings, sparking public outrage.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: On June 28, 2005, Joan Porco attends the trial of her son Christopher, accused of murdering his father and attacking her. Despite her initial identification and later refusal to testify, Christopher is found guilty based on circumstantial evidence.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: Murder by the Mob depicts the battle for justice by Peppino Impastato's family after his 1978 death was misclassified. His mother and brother reopened the case, leading to mafia boss Gaetano Badalamenti's 2002 conviction for the murder.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: In 1991, Ray and Faye Copeland, the oldest death row couple in the USA, are convicted of murdering five drifters. Police find the bodies after a tip-off. Faye's death sentence is commuted to life in 1999.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: In January 2002, Ashley Pond and Miranda Gaddis disappear in Portland. Neighbor Ward Weaver, a suspect with a violent history, is later found guilty of their murders and sentenced to life in prison.

programme.title: Naked Science: The Big Bang - The First Three Seconds
programme.desc: 13.7 billion years ago there was absolutely nothing. No space, no time, and no matter. Naked Science follows the first three seconds of creation when a speck of light appeared from nothing and rapidly expanded into an enormous Universe.

programme.title: Naked Science: Magnetic Earth
programme.desc: The Earth hurtles through space protected by an invisible force field, known as a magnetosphere.

programme.title: Earthrise: The First Lunar Voyage

programme.title: How to Colonize the Stars

programme.title: Hunting the Edge of Space: The Mystery of the Milky Way
programme.desc: The Mystery of the Milky Way chronicles the history of telescopes, from Galileo's refractor to Newton's reflector and beyond. This episode also looks at recent missions, including the Herschel Space Observatory's examination of the Milky Wa

programme.title: Arthur: The Pea and the Princess/D.W. and Dr. Whosit

programme.title: Warbirds of WWII
programme.desc: During World War II, the United States Army Air Forces (USAAF) played a pivotal role in the Allied victory. The USAAF fielded a diverse and technologically advanced aircraft, including the iconic B-17 Flying Fortress and the B-29 Superfortress.

programme.title: Hitler Uncovering His Fatal Obsession: Barbarossa Unleased 1941
programme.desc: E1 begins with a brief examination of the rationale of hatred projected by Hitler onto the Soviet-Slavic-Jewish world. Then it leaps forward, to see Hitler initiate an invasion of the USSR.

programme.title: Hitler Uncovering His Fatal Obsession: Time is Blood 1942-5
programme.desc: Episode 2 begins after the desperate German winter of 1941. Yet 1942 began badly for the Soviets, with more vicious Nazi campaigning seeing millions more Russians captured and starved.

programme.title: Hitler and Stalin: A Secret Relationship: Mistrust and Mutual Fascination
programme.desc: We have long ignored the "intimate" relationship between Hitler and Stalin, this mixture of mistrust and mutual fascination.

programme.title: Hitler and Stalin: A Secret Relationship: Two Tyrants
programme.desc: With the German-Soviet pact, Stalin and Hitler put aside all political logic to let the Western democracies believe that such a rapprochement was impossible.

programme.title: Hitler's British Island
programme.desc: The killings carried out by the Nazis on Alderney during World War II represent the worst mass murder in British history, yet no one has been brought to justice.

programme.title: Greatest Escapes of WWII
programme.desc: An adrenaline-fuelled dash through nazi-occupied Europe known as ‘The Flight of the Crow’, a death-defying escape across the world’s most perilous mountain range, and the largest escape of world war two - by men who chose death over dishonour.

programme.title: Guns That Changed The Game: If You Wish For Peace, Prepare For War
programme.desc: If You Wish For Peace, Prepare For War: Small arms production hit a boom in the early 1800s with the emergence of the modern cartridge; an invention that led to the lethal package.

programme.title: Combat Ships

programme.title: Married to Medicine
programme.desc: Quad jets off to California with Simone and Jill; Jackie struggles to tend to her ailing father both as a daughter and a doctor; Lisa throws a cooking party for her son's birthday, then drops a bomb that shocks Heavenly and Toya.

programme.title: Married to Medicine
programme.desc: Quad refocuses her energy on marketing her doggy fashion line; Heavenly's husband insists she's spending too much time away from home; Simone withdraws Lisa Nicole's invitation to her party.

programme.title: Married to Medicine
programme.desc: Determined to show she's more than a housewife, Toya follows through with her plan to market her husband's business; Quad has her first pop-up shop to bring Picture Perfect Pup to the forefront; Toya shares a rumor that makes Simone cry.

programme.title: Married to Medicine
programme.desc: Toya scouts locations for her husband's medical concierge business; Dr. Heavenly's daughter interns for Lisa Nicole; Lisa Nicole accuses Quad of questioning her husband's sexuality in public.

programme.title: Married to Medicine
programme.desc: Dr. Heavenly is ready to launch the dating app, but her husband does not like that she will be even busier; Jill throws a wild Prohibition Party; Dr. Simone proposes a trip to the Bahamas.

programme.title: Married to Medicine

programme.title: Million Dollar Listing Los Angeles
programme.desc: Altman and Tracy fight with stubborn sellers in a spiraling market when their high-end listings go nowhere; Altman debates on walking away from a client.

programme.title: Million Dollar Listing Los Angeles
programme.desc: Tracy attempts to rescue a pair of first-time home buyers who have buyer's remorse; Flagg visits the childhood dream house he purchased sight unseen; Altman is swimming in marble as he tries to move a mega-mansion in Pelican Crest.

programme.title: Million Dollar Listing Los Angeles
programme.desc: Tracy deals with heartbreak in her personal and professional life; Altman bags a huge listing, but it comes with a side of headache; Flagg agrees to list a property in Trousdale for more than it's worth, which he quickly comes to regret.

programme.title: Million Dollar Listing Los Angeles

programme.title: Million Dollar Listing Los Angeles

programme.title: Mayday Air Disaster

programme.title: Mayday Air Disaster
programme.desc: Air Ontario Flight #1363 prepares for takeoff after a brief layover. It reaches takeoff speed but struggles to get airborne, starts shaking violently, and crashes into the woods.

programme.title: Mayday Air Disaster
programme.desc: Air Inter Flight #148 starts its descent. Slightly off course, a controller guides the pilots, but as they descend the aircraft rips through the treetops below and slams into the side of a mountain.

programme.title: Mayday Air Disaster

programme.title: Mayday Air Disaster
programme.desc: Making a routine one-hour journey from Berlin, Crossair Flight #3597 is on its final approach to Zurich. But just minutes before landing the pilots cannot find the runway.

programme.title: A New Life In The Sun

programme.title: A New Life In The Sun
programme.desc: A holiday home business in France struggles with expensive maintenance costs. In Mallorca, a British-run adventure company deals with a very nervous cliff jumper, a couple of Brits in Spain spend two years’ worth of savings in eight weeks.

programme.title: A New Life In The Sun
programme.desc: A spectacular new excursion brings big earnings to a British run adventure company in Mallorca, a couple of expat-wannabe-caterers in France struggle to feed the crowds, and a couple from Essex open a glamping site in France.

programme.title: A New Life In The Sun
programme.desc: At a glamping site in France, mouldy tents cause worries for the British owners. The owners of a Mallorcan adventure company make a massive investment, and there’s a godsend for two British restaurateurs in France.

programme.title: A New Life In The Sun
programme.desc: A Norfolk couple moves to a picturesque French village to take over the only hotel, a Northumberland chef’s dream to open a restaurant in Mallorca, and two Belfast twenty-somethings gamble all their savings on buying a bar on the Costa del Sol.

programme.desc: When an astronaut/scientist discovers an alien embryo in a strange alien pod, he asks the celebrated Dr. Piranoid to come up to the space station to examine it -- not knowing that Piranoid plans to gene-slam Manta-ray DNA with alien DNA.

programme.title: Massive Monster Mayhem
programme.desc: Master Mayhem is packing for a little vacation! He found a super cheap hotel, and while he’s away, the Earth will pay! He’s sending Macho Cheese to crumble Earth’s Champion and cover our planet in cheese… and cheesy jokes!

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Bop Til You Drop - Sharing

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Easy as ABC
programme.desc: When Baby Bop uses the caboose mailbox to deliver a special invitation to her tea party, she discovers that no one can read her handwriting. Barney and his friends find creative ways to help her learn her ABCs!

programme.title: Barney and Friends: All About Me
programme.desc: Baby Bop and BJ have fun playing ball in the park. Barney helps them learn that trying something new can also be fun. Baby Bop prepares a special picnic for BJ, but much to his surprise, it consists mostly of her favorite foods.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Red, Yellow and Blue!
programme.desc: The kids have put together a "Caboose Color Collection" which showcases the primary colors—red, yellow and blue. Barney and Baby Bop enjoy songs and games suggested by the colorful objects.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Bienvenido, Barney!
programme.desc: Baby Bop's friend Monty the butterfly passes through the park with a flock of friends on their way to Mexico. When Monty gets left behind by his insect friends and family, Barney takes everyone on a visit to Mexico.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: Cousin Riff is moving to town. Barney encourages everyone to put on a special show.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: Barney can't stop snacking on pistachios! Barney helps BJ explore the deeper meaning of team sports and friendships.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Seeing - Beethoven's Hear!
programme.desc: An ordinary game of "hide and seek" takes a not-so-ordinary turn when Baby Bop claims that an elephant is hiding in the park! Barney and Riff are happy to play along, but BJ thinks that his sister may be "seeing things."

programme.desc: Burne assigns April a story reporting on the increase in urban crime. The only problem is that urban crime is decreasing.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Sleuth on the Loose
programme.desc: While the Turtles are watching their favorite TV show April interrupts the broadcast with a special bulletin about a break-in at Dynamic Science Labs.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Rust Never Sleeps
programme.desc: Krang works on an oxidizer rocket with enough power to blast the Technodrome to Earth, but something goes wrong, and instead of thrust, the device grows rust.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Venice on the Half-Shell

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Artless

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Ring of Fire

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): The Irish Jig Is Up

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Shredder's New Sword
programme.desc: In Dimension X Krang is using a long-range scanner and discovers a rare metal buried in a ruined castle in England. The metal turns out to be King Arthur's legendary sword, Excalibur.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): The Lost Queen of Atlantis
programme.desc: Shredder, Rocksteady and Bebop are searching for a valuable energy source in Athens and discover that it is April's new necklace.

programme.desc: Nutty Hill is suffering from a stifling heat wave. Searching in vain for a bit of cool air, Grizzy suddenly has a brilliant idea.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Just as Grizzly was about to coat a nice big fresh salmon with chocolate spread and snuggle up in front of the TV, he discovers that the pantry is empty.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzy's nap is interrupted by an annoying vibration, the forest ranger's cell phone. It has various functions, including a game to which he instantly becomes addicted.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Freed by the thawing ice, a mysterious giant egg slides up to the front door of the log cabin and the Lemmings watch with interest as it hatches.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzy decides to slim down with the ranger's bodybuilding equipment, but before starting his exercise routine, he will first have to settle the problem of the Lemmings, who are already using the machines as an amusement park.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: By chance, Grizzly discovers that the ranger's new robot vacuum cleaner also gives very pleasant back massages.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzy discovers a gadget that controls various functions in the cabin. He will finally be able to get rid of the Lemmings, without even leaving the couch.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: To make sure that the Lemmings don't snatch his chocolate spread, Grizzy turns the fridge into a safe. However, the Lemmings see Grizzy open the door while sleepwalking.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: The Lemmings have invented a new popcorn game which consists of throwing popcorn and catching it with their mouths.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzly wants to learn to dance the mambo to please his lovely she-bear. He finds a learning kit at the ranger's home which has a dance carpet with footprints.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: After receiving a terrible blow on the head, Grizzly thinks he's a rodent and tries to get the gang to adopt him.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzy is exhausted, and he goes to fetch a pillow and a sleeping mask, but the Lemmings have entirely different plans for the couch.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzy discovers that the frenzied breakdance the Lemmings are doing is actually due to the fleas they are infested with.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Another heat wave, and even the riverbed is dried up. One very thirsty Grizzly discovers that the Lemmings have used the last remaining bottles of water to fill their inflatable pool.

programme.title: Pokemon: Advanced Battle
programme.desc: May is going after her fifth Contest Ribbon to qualify for the Grand Festival, but when her shady rival Harley teams up with Team Rocket, things don’t look good!

programme.title: Pokemon: Advanced Battle

programme.title: Pokemon: Advanced Battle

programme.title: Pokemon: Advanced Battle

programme.title: Pokemon: Advanced Battle

programme.title: Pokemon: Advanced Battle

programme.title: Pokemon: Advanced Battle

programme.title: Pokemon: Advanced Battle

programme.title: Teletubbies

programme.desc: Danni's relationship with an unlikely person develops in an unexpected twist. Aaron recruits Andi's help. Sabrina tries to figure out the shift in Karen's attitude. (S1, ep9)

programme.title: Tyler Perry's Sistas: Revelations
programme.desc: Andi's suspicions are raised concerning her lover. Sabrina and Calvin start to make progress. Danni still has a chip on her shoulder, but sets it aside in favour of helping Andi. (S1, ep10)

programme.title: The Game: Away Game
programme.desc: Derwin invites Melanie to his first away game in Miami, but due to her heavy school load, she decides to pass.

programme.title: The Game: The Rules Of The Game
programme.desc: As a favor to Derwin, Melanie attends the first Saber Sunbeam meeting of the season, only to learn that unlike the wives, football players "girlfriends" are not considered legitimate and are treated as such by the other wives.

programme.title: The Game: Gifted
programme.desc: When Melanie sends gifts to the football team on behalf of Derwin in honor of the first game, Derwin gets teased and his teammates Jason and Malik feel pressured to reciprocate. A gift giving competition ensues.

programme.title: The Game: How Tasha Got Her Groove Back
programme.desc: When Malik tells Tasha that he is having trouble with the offensive coordinator, Kenny, he asks her to speak with him.

programme.title: The Game: Brittany's Super Sweet Sixth
programme.desc: When Kelley's daughter tells her she wants an extravagant birthday party like the rest of her friends, Kelly approaches Jason for money and he refuses.

programme.title: The Game: Rift And Separate
programme.desc: A minor argument escalates into a major locker room rigt between Jason and Malik that affects their playing relationship on the field.

programme.title: The Game: Mi Casa Es Su Casa
programme.desc: When Tasha returns early from a trip she finds Malik throwing a wild party at their house and shuts it down.

programme.title: Paranormal State: The Name
programme.desc: Ryan Buell and company set out on one of their most dangerous cases to date - to help a single mother who is experiencing paranormal activity in a home that was once the site of brutal murder.

programme.title: Paranormal State: The Asylum
programme.desc: PRS explores a New York State Prison that was once a mental asylum. Ryan and his team go behind bars to uncover the institution's paranormal past and present.

programme.title: Paranormal State: Desperate Households
programme.desc: When Julie and her family moved into their dream home in suburban New Jersey they quickly realized they were not alone.

programme.title: Paranormal State: Vendetta
programme.desc: P.R.S. investigates claims of supernatural revenge in the Pennsylvania Poconos. When a client's new boyfriend moves in, does the paranormal move in with him? Or did her ex put a hex on her house and the entire family?

programme.title: Paranormal State: Lost Souls
programme.desc: PRS and Psychic/medium Michelle Belanger investigate a Pennsylvania home; where a mother named Kathy and her son Michael have both felt the presence of a young boy and hear voices calling, "Mom?" but no one is there.

programme.title: Paranormal State: Hide and Seek
programme.desc: Hillary and her nine-year-old son Hunter are being harassed by a shadow presence in their Victorian home in Newburgh, Indiana. Trap doors in the basement reveal artifacts from a bygone era, and the clients have recorded persistent strange noises.

programme.title: Paranormal State: Woman in the Window
programme.desc: Ryan and his team lend paranormal assistance to a family who lives in a historic home that was once a part of the Underground Railroad. Does the answer to their haunting lie in the mysterious tunnels beneath the house?

programme.title: Paranormal State: The Knickerbocker
programme.desc: PRS checks into the Knickerbocker Hotel in Linesville, Pennsylvania where the owners are reporting strange noises and the appearance of phantom children in some of the rooms.

programme.title: Paranormal State: Laws of Attraction
programme.desc: When Paula's young autistic son Michael, with limited verbal skills uttered the words "Go away scary dead boy" -- it sent a chill up her spine.

programme.title: Paranormal State: Haunted Homecoming
programme.desc: Ryan brings PRS to his hometown of Sumter, SC to investigate a chilling case where a young boy was tragically shot and killed on a front doorstep while trick-or-treating on Halloween.

programme.title: Most Haunted: Codnor Castle Part 2
programme.desc: We stay with the team and find out what happened after we left them last time. The foreboding Codnor Castle Cottage maintains its attack on the Most Haunted team with dramatic effect causing Yvette calls an early end to their night in Hell House.

programme.title: Most Haunted: Birmingham Central
programme.desc: Yvette takes her team to a disused prison in Birmingham which has housed some of the most notorious criminals in British history.

programme.title: Most Haunted: Dudley Castle
programme.desc: The ruins that make up Dudley Castle house a plethora of paranormal activity for Yvette and her team to investigate. Strange noises and a violent poltergeist await them as they turn the lights out and do battle with the unseen forces.

programme.title: Most Haunted: Llanfyliln House Part 1
programme.desc: Workhouses were not the best of places to be, so why would anyone stay after they had died? Yvette takes her team on a memorable journey to find out. For a few team members it was a night they will never forget as they are pushed to the very limits.

programme.title: Most Haunted: Llanfyliln House Part 2
programme.desc: Whatever was making itself known to the team earlier, continues in this episode as something seems to be following Yvette from the shadows.

programme.title: Auction Hunters: Auction Hunters, Ink.

programme.title: Auction Hunters: The Chi-Town Showdown

programme.title: Auction Hunters: Cashville, Tennessee

programme.title: Auction Hunters: Carolyn Goes Topless

programme.desc: Allen & Ton try to get the inside scoop on their competition by chatting up local auction buyers. The guys find some heavy gold-mining machinery from the 1950s & an amateur rocket that can travel faster than the speed of sound!

programme.desc: Ton and Allen race back from an auction to purchase a rare comic valued at over $100,000. The guys uncover a Samurai sword used for ritual suicide and Allen lives out his childhood fantasy of becoming knight.

programme.title: Criminal Confessions

programme.title: Criminal Confessions

programme.title: Criminal Confessions
programme.desc: After an elderly woman comes into a large sum of money, she is attacked. Investigators soon uncover a twisted tale of addiction, brutality and betrayal.

programme.title: Criminal Confessions

programme.title: Criminal Confessions

programme.title: Murdertown

programme.title: Murdertown

programme.title: Myth Hunters
programme.desc: According to legend, King Arthur was Britain’s greatest king. He led his Knights of the Round Table from Camelot, a spectacular castle, and fought Anglo-Saxon invaders, uniting the country. Leslie Alcock led a team to find Camelot.

programme.title: Myth Hunters
programme.desc: A local newspaper editor and businessman in South Africa unwittingly finds himself at the epicentre of a legend of buried gold that began during the Boer War.

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett: JFK
programme.desc: Richard meets with some prominent JFK assassination researchers to discuss the November 1963 murder of President John F. Kennedy.

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett: RFID Chips
programme.desc: Could implantable RFID chips or Radio-frequency identification tags currently in widespread use in retail industry be used to surreptitiously track and control people?

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett: Remote Viewing
programme.desc: We meet several individuals who claim they have the ability to transcend time and space and identify people, objects, and locations hidden from their view.

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett: The Rise of the Fourth Reich (Nazi's in America)
programme.desc: The Allies defeated the Axis Powers on the battlefield, but did they actually win the war? Richard interviews several alternative historians who say that while the German Army may have surrendered in 1945, the Nazi's never officially did.

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett: The Murder of Marilyn Monroe
programme.desc: Did this pop icon and blond bombshell succumb to a drug overdose in 1962 as we've been led to believe or was she killed for 'knowing too much?"

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett
programme.desc: Richard interviews the expedition organizer who is planning on leading a team of scientists, filmmakers and tourists to the Arctic aboard a Russian nuclear ice-breaker in search of an entrance into the interior of our world.

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett: The Shroud of Turin
programme.desc: Is this famous piece of linen cloth the actual burial cloth of Jesus Christ or a clever Medieval hoax?

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: In January 2002, Ashley Pond and Miranda Gaddis disappear in Portland. Neighbor Ward Weaver, a suspect with a violent history, is later found guilty of their murders and sentenced to life in prison.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: A picture-perfect Canadian couple Karla Homolka and Paul Bernardo join to perform torture and mutilation on young victims - crimes that are virtually unspeakable. Two teenage girls were raped and murdered, and the couple even filmed themselves doing it.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: On June 27, 2005, Dennis Rader, known as the BTK killer, pleads guilty to ten murders he committed in Wichita, Kansas, from 1974 to 1994. After a journalist's article reignites the case, Rader is arrested and sentenced to ten life terms without parole.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: In September 1987, Simon Dale is murdered. His ex-wife, Susan Wilberforce, is acquitted of the crime but later convicted of fraud and sentenced to seven years. The murder remains unsolved.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: In January 2002, fashion journalist Christa Worthington is found murdered in her Truro home. Christopher McCowen is convicted in 2006 of her murder, robbery, and rape, despite his claims of innocence and other DNA found at the scene.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: In 1999, NFL player Rae Carruth is convicted for orchestrating the shooting of his pregnant girlfriend. He is sentenced to 18-24 years. Their son, Chancellor, has cerebral palsy.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: In February 1977, Australian heiress Janie Shepherd vanishes in London. David Lashley, a convicted rapist, later confesses to her murder in prison. In 1990, he is convicted based on this confession and sentenced to life.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: In 1998, Peter Bergna's car crashes off a mountain, killing his wife Rinette. After suspicions arise and a first trial ends in a hung jury, Bergna is convicted of murder in 2002 following new testimony about his abusive behavior.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: In 1975, 15-year-old Martha Moxley is murdered in Belle Haven, Connecticut. The case goes cold until 2002, when Michael Skakel, a family member, is convicted based on new evidence. Skakel maintains his innocence.

programme.title: Bang Goes The Theory
programme.desc: The origins of weather; seasons allow life to form; Jem checks out wave power in Scotland.

programme.title: Arthur: The Making of Arthur/Dancing Fools
programme.desc: When Matt Damon puts out a call for homemade videos for his TV show, he sets off a frantic rush as Arthur and his gang film their lives. Mrs. Molina is a teaching a dance class for kids. Will Francine and George be the next Fred and Ginger?

programme.title: Arthur: Buster's Breathless/The Fright Stuff
programme.desc: Will Buster's friends treat him the same after they learn he has asthma? Prankster have arrived in Elwood City--just in time for the "Scare Your Pants Off" costume party! It's boys vs. girls in what becomes a great big battle of the pranks.

programme.title: Arthur: My Club Rules/Stolen Bike
programme.desc: The gang can't agree on rules for their club, and each starts his own. Next, Francine's embarrassed by her crummy old bike. Why can't she have a shiny new one like all her friends?

programme.title: Arthur: The Chronicles of Buster/On this Spot
programme.desc: Buster learns that special effects can't take the place of hard work when conquering real mountains. Arthur is thrilled to find out that Sitting Bull once passed through town! He rallies the residents to mark the spot with a monument.

programme.title: Arthur: Buster Spaces Out/The Long Road Home
programme.desc: Inspired by footage of the moon landing, Buster wants to go to outer space someday! For now, it'd be cool to build and launch a rocket! Then, after costing Lakewood Elementary the win in a relay race, George wants to redeem himself.

programme.title: Arthur: Arthur's Family Vacation/Grandpa Dave's Old Country Farm
programme.desc: What's got moldy bathrooms, lumpy beds and lots of rain? Arthur's family vacation. Then, Arthur and D.W. go to visit their grandfather, but the visit doesn't turn out as expected, because Grandpa Dave isn't your usual kind of grandfather.

programme.title: Arthur: Arthur's First Sleepover/Arthur's New Year's Eve
programme.desc: Have you ever been afraid of the dark? Despite D.W.'s threat of aliens, Arthur sets his tent for his first sleepover. Then, what exactly happens at midnight, December 31st? There are plenty of rumors, but no one seems to know for sure.

programme.title: Arthur: Binky Barnes, Wingman/To Beat Or Not To Beat
programme.desc: Does Binky have a favorite pastime? Not until citywide Bug Week, when suddenly all he can think about is butterflies. Then, there's a horrible racket in Elwood City, and it's Francine practicing her singing and drumming for the Talent Show!

programme.title: Hitler and Stalin: A Secret Relationship: Two Tyrants
programme.desc: With the German-Soviet pact, Stalin and Hitler put aside all political logic to let the Western democracies believe that such a rapprochement was impossible.

programme.title: Hitler's Last Secrets: Fatal Liaison - The Strange Affair of Adolf and Eva
programme.desc: The protracted romance between Adolf Hitler and Eva Braun began in 1929 and ended in 1945 in the bunker under the ruins of the Chancellery.

programme.title: The Ultimate History of the Messerschmitt
programme.desc: In this program we look at the development of the Bf 109 which flew in all theaters of conflict in which the Luftwaffe was involved. More Bf 109s were built than any other fighter of the era.

programme.title: Hitler's Propaganda Machine: Building The Brand
programme.desc: Hitler’s fascination with propaganda was born in the trenches of the First World War. There he saw it emerge as a potent tool of psychological warfare - one that he would harness to build the Nazi movement. Witness Hitler's early propaganda tactics.

programme.title: Hitler's Propaganda Machine: Power and Persuasion
programme.desc: This episode explores Hitler's approach to propaganda in power; where persuasion was achieved through propaganda and terror, all in service of one goal: the creation of a state under one shared ideology. A new German power fueled by propaganda.

programme.title: Secret Nazi Expeditions: Walking with Witches
programme.desc: Convinced the medieval witch trials were a Judeo-Christian attempt to exterminate the Aryans, Himmler established a special team led by Reinhard Heydrich to build a database of medieval German witchcraft practices.

programme.title: Guns That Changed The Game: The Gods Of War
programme.desc: The Gods of War: The industrial revolution changed it all when it came to cannons as they evolved from wholly inaccurate and immobile to deadly killing machines.

programme.title: Secret Wars Uncovered: Invasion of Panama
programme.desc: Invasion of Panama: In 1989, 20,000 US troops entered Panama to overthrow military dictator Manuel Noriega and around 500 civilians were killed. What prompted the invasion?

programme.title: Hitler's Engineers
programme.desc: As one of their most controversial scientists, Heisenberg was key in the Nazis' efforts to build an atomic bomb. How close was he to achieving his goal?

programme.title: Magi-Nation
programme.desc: Venturing into a Shadow Geyser in their pursuit of Korg and Zed – who transformed Orwin into a Shadow Magi and took him down to the Core to serve Agram - Tony, Edyn and Strag drop into a nightmarish maze inside the geyser.

programme.title: Storm Hawks
programme.desc: Having lost the Aurora Stone to the cunning Dark Ace, The Storm Hawks mount a daring mission, sneaking into the very heart of the evil empire of Cyclonia to take the powerful crystal back.

programme.title: Massive Monster Mayhem
programme.desc: Master Mayhem receives a visit from… himself! From the future?! With two MM’s in the mix, Earth is destined for doom... if they could just stop arguing. Meanwhile RoBro flexes his mechanical muscles in a Monster Mashdown that’ll go down in history.

programme.title: MetaJets
programme.desc: A new group of aerial daredevils, the Stunt-Hawks, have arrived for a series of shows in Soaring Heights, which coincides with a string of daring aerial heists throughout the city.

programme.title: Magi-Nation
programme.desc: In Naroom's Arena Ruins, Tony, Edyn and Strag are running battle drills in an attempt to master their still-raw Final Dreamer powers... when Furok begins to act strangely.

programme.title: Transformers: Armada: Tactician
programme.desc: A new transformer enters the solar system. On the moon, Thrust the master tactician arrives to assist Megatron in his battle against the Autobots, but all does not go according to plan.

programme.title: MetaJets
programme.desc: On the eve of a solar eclipse, General Raven travels to the great Pyramids of Egypt, to locate the artifact that will trigger an ancient Egyptian solar weapon capable of taking out any target.

programme.title: Transformers: Armada: Dash
programme.desc: While the battle continues, Hot Shot goes to Cybertron to try to convince Galvatron of the impending danger. Thrust's true allegiance and agenda is revealed.

programme.title: Yu-Gi-Oh! VRAINS: Standing Tall
programme.desc: The Tower of Hanoi is nearly complete – leaving Varis a win away from deleting LINK VRAINS once and for all!

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Seeing - Beethoven's Hear!
programme.desc: An ordinary game of "hide and seek" takes a not-so-ordinary turn when Baby Bop claims that an elephant is hiding in the park! Barney and Riff are happy to play along, but BJ thinks that his sister may be "seeing things."

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: When she finds out that Daniel is going on a trip the next day, Baby Bop wants to go on a trip as well. Everyone decides to pretend to travel the same way Daniel will travel.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: That Makes Me Mad!
programme.desc: Barney helps his young friends find good ways to express and deal with angry emotions.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: At Home in the Park
programme.desc: The children work together to make a bird house. They are happy to learn that the bird will have lots of company; many animals make their homes in the park too!

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: Baby Bop has trouble following the rules and taking turns while playing games. BJ hurts his toe while pretending to be Captain Pickles.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Look What I Can Do
programme.desc: Barney's friends are playing a fun game of "I Can Do" where they each share something they can do. When BJ suggests having an "I Can Do" show, Baby Bop becomes worried.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: The Blame Game - Airplanes
programme.desc: Marcos accidentally breaks an art show project that Melanie has made. Afraid that she will be angry, he doesn't say anything, but then feels guilty. Barney helps him see that honesty and taking responsibility for what we do is always best. together.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: My Baby Brother
programme.desc: Barney reminds Baby Bop and BJ that although babies start out little, they grow fast. Through pretend play, favorite songs and fun games, they learn about the many ways we grow every day.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Movement - Separation
programme.desc: When some children in the park invite Barney to join them for a race, BJ and Baby Bop decide he'll need their help. They show him several ways to get ready for the big race by warming up and stretching.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): The Lost Queen of Atlantis
programme.desc: Shredder, Rocksteady and Bebop are searching for a valuable energy source in Athens and discover that it is April's new necklace.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Turtles on the Orient Express
programme.desc: April is covering the revival of the Orient Express. Shredder is on the train with Rocksteady and Bebop carrying the supercharger, a device to accelerate the train to super speed.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): April Gets in Dutch
programme.desc: April is covering the showing of the world-famed Duchess Diamond. Krang wants the diamond, a flawless stone, to focus the rays on his laser dimension blade.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Northern Lights Out
programme.desc: Eric Red seizes both April and Professor Swenson's map to the underground volcanoes in Scandinavia to tap into the molten energy and melt the polar ice caps.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Elementary, My Dear Turtle
programme.desc: The Turtles are in England, when they are taken back to the past, via an experimental atomic clock and must team up with Sherlock Holmes and Doctor Watson in order to get the clock back and return to the present.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Night of the Dark Turtle
programme.desc: When Donatello receives a jolt of electrical energy, he undergoes a radical personality transformation, turning into 'The Dark Turtle', a psychotic caped crimefighter on a mission of revenge against Shredder!

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): The Starchild
programme.desc: The Turtles face a crisis of galactic proportions when they must play babysitter to an alien infant with incredible powers.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): The Legend of Koji
programme.desc: The Turtles take an incredible trip back in time when they follow Shredder back to 16th Century Japan, where they join forces with Splinter's ancestor, the youthful samurai koji, and help him found the Foot Clan.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Convicts from Dimension X
programme.desc: Donatello, tinkering with his InterDimensional Early Warning System device, accidently frees two vicious convicts from their maximum security cell on Dimension X, bringing them to earth.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): White Belt, Black Heart
programme.desc: Splinter's ancient sensei, Master Mogo-San, arrives from Japan, seeking to find his grandson, to hand him a precious family heirloom - a sacred white ninja belt.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Another heat wave, and even the riverbed is dried up. One very thirsty Grizzly discovers that the Lemmings have used the last remaining bottles of water to fill their inflatable pool.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: The Lemmings have caught a little spider. Her sticky threads are perfect for bungee jumping. Grizzly, who has no intention of getting caught in the web, decides to crush the insect.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzy and the Lemmings fight over chocolate-spread jar lids, which they use as chips to win goodies. But more importantly, there's a grand prize, a giant jar of Yummy.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzy has prepared himself a TV dinner to eat in front of his favourite series, but his moment of pleasure is abruptly interrupted by unexplained static.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: The Lemmings are having a rave party. When Grizzy throws their stereo into the river, he sparks a general power outage and is unable to watch his favourite shows.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: When he realises that his beloved she-bear adores the smell of flowers, Grizzy decides to offer her the geraniums that decorate the window ledges of the ranger's cabin.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: After making a fool of himself in front of the she-bear, Grizzly is ousted from the cabin by the Lemmings. But Grizzly's luck is about to change.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzy can't believe his eyes when he sees objects moving all by themselves. He soon realises it's the Lemmings who are playing around with a magnet.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzy is persuaded that the she-bear he's in love with has been turned into a frog. Whilst he looks through books of fairy tales, searching for a means to return his beloved to her original form, the Lemmings try to recover the frog.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzly discovers a baby raccoon not far from the log cabin. When he sees its big moist eyes, Grizzly melts and adopts the creature. When the Lemmings drag the baby into their dangerous games, the overprotective Grizzly steps in.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzy discovers the pleasures of video games on the ranger's console. The Lemmings want to try their hand at the joystick but Grizzy refuses to interrupt his kart racing game.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzy builds a look-alike robot to keep the Lemmings away while he prepares a party for his beloved she-bear. But his double's artificial intelligence leaves a lot to be desired.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzly's she-bear is convinced she's a lemming after hitting her head. Grizzly believes another well-aimed hit will correct her thinking, but the Lemmings are thrilled to have a new playmate.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: When a meteorite falls not far from the log cabin, strange berries start to grow. One of their properties is that they make anyone who eats them grow to disproportionate sizes.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Under the wood floor of the log cabin, Grizzly and the Lemmings discover the entrance to an underground gallery with an old treasure card that indicates the location of a treasure.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: When he brandishes an Indian sceptre that the Lemmings brought home with other antique objects, Grizzy sees the Lemmings bow low before him and behave like servants.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzy tries to impress the she-bear that he's in love with by making a sculpture of her from a piece of wood. But as soon as the artist's back is turned, the Lemmings gnaw at the work of art in order to transform it into a toboggan slide.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: The Lemmings throw a surprise birthday party for one of the clan. When Happy Birthday rings out, the surprise is as great for Grizzly, who is trying to have a nap after a hearty meal, as it is for the birthday Lemming hero in question.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: A gust of wind sweeps away the flower that Grizzly's adored she-bear wears in her hair. Grizzly sees this as a golden opportunity to dazzle her by bringing it back.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzy finds two identical vases that are actually communicating vessels in a Native American Indian chest. If he sticks his hand in one, it comes out the other, regardless of the distance between them.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzy finds a vintage stopwatch in the garage which lets one wind back the minute that has just passed by, and it can even stop time and movement in one's surroundings.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzy is disturbed in the middle of his TV snack by a flow of incongruous objects that have been transformed into hot-air balloons.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzy discovers with great delight that the ranger has purchased a brand new multi-function Stressless recliner. But the Lemmings transform it into a fearsome multi-game chair.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: The Lemmings play kite surf with a homemade kite. Grizzly notices that the she-bear he's in love with watches the kite perform arabesques in the sky with admiration.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzy snatches the last jar of chocolate spread out from under the noses of the Lemmings. In exchange, he offers them the toy stuck inside the lid, a mini-flute.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: The Lemmings play a new game, floating like flags in the breeze from the fan set on maximum. But Grizzy quickly rids the living room of this interference.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: The Lemmings have a grand old time tearing down their Kapla block buildings, which they rebuild just as fast. Grizzly remarks that the she-bear he's in love with is impressed by the Lemmings' construction and makes believe that it's his work of art.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: The Lemmings draw inspiration from lessons they find in a martial arts book and start breaking every wooden object they can get their hands on into two. When Grizzy tries to stop them, they go on to the next lesson and get him in a judo hold.

programme.title: Pokemon: Advanced Battle
programme.desc: Our heroes meet an aspiring comedian named Audrey and her showbiz sidekick and Pokémon partner, Chatot.

programme.title: Pokemon: Advanced Battle
programme.desc: Our heroes find themselves embroiled in Pokémon politics as two Weavile face off for leadership of a group of Sneasel!

programme.title: Pokemon: Advanced Battle
programme.desc: When our heroes arrive at the Battle Pyramid for Ash’s battle with Brandon, they discover a Nurse Joy who’s a big fan of Ash and May!

programme.title: Pokemon: Advanced Battle
programme.desc: After their recent defeats in important matches at the Battle Pyramid and the Grand Festival, Ash and May are feeling a little down, until several old friends come together to support them.

programme.title: Pokemon: Advanced Battle
programme.desc: When Nurse Joy becomes too ill to take care of the Pokémon Center, our heroes pitch in, coming to the aid of a Pokésitter and his charges after a bus accident.

programme.title: Pokemon: Advanced Battle
programme.desc: Ash prepares for his final rematch with Brandon at the Battle Pyramid by rounding up the Pokémon he’s been with the longest, spending his time sharing old memories with them and training intensely.

programme.title: Pokemon: Advanced Battle
programme.desc: Ash has lost to Brandon in two previous battles, and now his Charizard has been defeated! Can he battle back to claim the final Frontier Symbol?

programme.title: Pokemon: Advanced Battle
programme.desc: Ash has beaten the Battle Frontier, but his competitive spirit won’t let him rest for long, and he and May both sign up for an unofficial Pokémon Contest in Terracotta Town!

programme.title: Pokemon: Advanced Battle
programme.desc: After one last contest battle between Ash and May, our heroes go their separate ways, and Ash discovers his next adventure!

programme.title: Pokemon: Indigo League
programme.desc: Ash Ketchum, a 10-year-old from Pallet Town, is finally old enough to receive his first Pokémon! However, he oversleeps, and the only remaining Pokémon at Professor Oak’s lab is a strong-willed Pikachu!

programme.title: Pokemon: Indigo League
programme.desc: Ash rushes into Viridian City with his wounded Pikachu, where he encounters Jessie, James, and Meowth of Team Rocket, as well as Misty, who’s angry about Ash wrecking her bike!

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies dance in and out of the trees. The Teletubbies watch Matthew, James and Mark feeding their chickens. There's toast all over Teletubbyland when the Tubby Toaster makes too much toast.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: A balloon appears in Teletubbyland; it floats in the air. The Teletubbies follow the balloon all over Teletubbyland, but they can't catch it.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Tubbies watch some children build towers of different shapes and sizes. Laa-Laa and Dipsy play an up and down game. They take turns to sit down and stand up, then they go up and down the slide.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies dance round the Dome, but stop to watch Zoe collect some things in her Treasure Box for her sister Sophie. In Teletubbyland, Dipsy hears magical music and sees a cloud.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies watch some children go for a ride on a Sea Tractor. Po goes for a ride on her scooter, while she’s riding she sees Laa-Laa’s ball, Dipsy’s hat and Tinky Winky’s bag. They’ve left them all behind!

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Tubbies play a bumping bottoms game. They stop to watch some children play some ball games. Laa-Laa’s ball goes in a puddle, she tries to get it back but can’t reach it.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies watch some children go on a boat trip to see some seals. Tinky Winky is wondering what everyone is doing.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies are very, very happy. They watch two boys make Chinese Lanterns with their uncle. In Teletubbyland, it is time for Tubby Toast, but where has Laa-Laa's toast gone?

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Andy Brown and some two children look for fox cubs. Tinky Winky falls asleep in bed, Dipsy falls asleep at the table, Laa-Laa falls asleep on the slide and Po falls asleep on the scooter.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Teletubbies love dancing, but they love children even more. So everything stops when they watch some children painting in a most unusual way. Back at the house, a trail leads Tinky Winky all around Teletubbyland to find out.

programme.title: Mona the Vampire
programme.desc: One person's junk is another person's treasure! But who, in their right mind, would throw away a perfectly good genie bottle? // During show-and-tell, Angela shows off the latest addition to her toy drawer: a line of rather creepy-looking designer dolls.

programme.title: Mona the Vampire
programme.desc: When Mona's parents accidentally sell her Book of the Slimy at their garage sale Mona has to get it back // Can a lovelorn vampire lady and a lonely-hearted werewolf find true love?

programme.title: Mona the Vampire
programme.desc: After a ventriloquist act gets big laughs at Mona's expense, she discovers that the dummy is alive // Mona notices a mysterious limousine cruising around town causing adults to lose their confidence about going to their high school reunion.

programme.title: Mona the Vampire
programme.desc: Mona and her pals meet their monster movie hero and discover that he REALLY lives the part. // Mrs. Bryerson's wedding to the local lawn bowling champ is in jeopardy

programme.title: Mona the Vampire

programme.title: Matlock: The Prisoner (Part 1)

programme.title: Icons Unearthed: The Lord of the Rings

programme.title: Icons Unearthed: The Lord of the Rings

programme.title: Icons Unearthed: The Lord of the Rings

programme.title: Icons Unearthed: The Lord of the Rings

programme.title: Icons Unearthed: The Lord of the Rings

programme.title: Relic Hunter: A Vanishing Art

programme.title: Monkey

programme.title: Monkey
programme.desc: Troubled by dreams of his dying mother, Tripitaka questions his duty. The journey teaches him balance between love and spiritual purpose.

programme.title: Monkey
programme.desc: Monkey steals Heaven’s peaches, gains immortality, and battles officials. All are cast to Earth: Monkey trapped under rocks, Pigsy and Sandy cursed. Buddha decrees a holy man must fetch scriptures from India to save the world.

programme.title: Monkey
programme.desc: Kuan-yin seeks a holy man for the scripture quest, choosing young priest Hsüan Tsang, now Tripitaka. With Monkey’s magic staff, his powers, and a talking horse, their journey from China to India begins.

programme.title: Monkey
programme.desc: Tripitaka and Monkey start their perilous quest. A village terrorized by Pigsy leads to battle, then they face Sandy the cannibal water monster. Sandstorms and tiger ghosts threaten—will Monkey save his master from doom?

programme.title: Monkey
programme.desc: The pilgrims meet Golden Horn and Silver Horn. Pigsy is bewitched, Monkey is tested by an old woman, and Tripitaka and Sandy are captured. A Taoist magician turns the tide when Golden Horn loses his magic bottle.

programme.title: Ironside

programme.desc: After Ironside talks to a criminology class, sniper fire wounds a student; with Brenda Scott, Peter Deuel.

programme.title: Ironside
programme.desc: Ironside and his staff baby-sit an abandoned infant who is the main clue to the identity of a would-be airplane bomber.

programme.title: Ironside
programme.desc: Brown is wrongly accused of police brutality in his apprehension of a girl who later died from head injuries.

programme.title: Ironside
programme.desc: Frustrated with police progress, Mark decides to solve his girlfriend's murder; with Dwayne Hickman, David Carradine.

programme.desc: Sometimes, when a man and a woman unite, a terrible urge for destruction can awaken in them both. Happy couples become deadly teams, intent on one goal: murder.

programme.title: Deadly Women
programme.desc: While marriage is meant to be for life, it often ends in divorce. These wives took their marriages vow seriously: "till death do us part."

programme.title: Happily Never After
programme.desc: Months after their wedding, Keith and Anita Yunk sleep peacefully in their bed when an attacker with a knife interrupts their slumber and hacks them to pieces.

programme.title: Happily Never After

programme.desc: High school volleyball player Jeanna Giese is fatigued and has numbness in her arm. When she experiences double-vision and loss of balance, doctors know it is far more sinister than a sports injury.

programme.title: Murder by the Sea
programme.desc: Felicity Paget was killed by a mystery knifeman who entered her house, and also attacked her fiancé William Webb. The fleeing attacker left footprints behind in blood.

programme.desc: Ryan brings PRS to his hometown of Sumter, SC to investigate a chilling case where a young boy was tragically shot and killed on a front doorstep while trick-or-treating on Halloween.

programme.title: Paranormal State: Smoke and Shadows
programme.desc: Sarah believes her Parkville, MD home has been invaded by a powerful force ever since her roommate Dennis moved in. PRS investigates claims that Dennis has been attacked by the entity, a possible poltergeist.

programme.title: Paranormal State: Permission To Do Harm
programme.desc: When a mirror levitates and shatters on its own, P.R.S. heeds the call to investigate a spirit that has been unleashed on an unsuspecting family. Ryan must track down the spirit and contain it before it does irreparable damage.

programme.title: Paranormal State: Sixth Sense
programme.desc: A worried mother calls the Paranormal Research Society to help her son Matthew, a boy who sees dead people. Matthew's story strikes a chord with team leader Ryan Buell who also experienced paranormal activity as a child.

programme.title: Paranormal State: Do Bad Things
programme.desc: After receiving a letter requesting PRS' help, Ryan and the team head to Ohio to help a grieving family and determine if the family's house contributed to the untimely death of a daughter.

programme.title: Paranormal State: Room & Board
programme.desc: The owners of a vacation lodge in the remote town of North Hudson, NY, feel their woodsy getaway is plagued by negative spirits.

programme.title: Paranormal State: Mothman
programme.desc: Ryan takes his team to Point Pleasant, West Virginia to investigate his own personal holy grail of paranormal lore: the mysterious Mothman.

programme.title: Paranormal State: The Firehouse
programme.desc: Everyone from the chief to the trainees at the Clementon Fire Department is convinced that the ghost of a former member is haunting their firehouse.

programme.title: Paranormal State: Dark Man
programme.desc: A grieving mother suspects her deceased son may have fallen victim to a mysterious entity called The Dark Man.

programme.title: Paranormal State: Room 37

programme.title: Paranormal State: Death Room

programme.title: Auction Hunters: Win Lose or Joust
programme.desc: Ton and Allen race back from an auction to purchase a rare comic valued at over $100,000. The guys uncover a Samurai sword used for ritual suicide and Allen lives out his childhood fantasy of becoming knight.

programme.title: Auction Hunters: Gone in 20 Seconds
programme.desc: Ton and Allen get taken down by their fiercest opponent, CJ, but a twist of fate keeps the guys in the game. They uncover a Geiger counter and NASCAR impact wrenches, while their accountant delivers some shocking news.

programme.title: Auction Hunters: Animal Instincts
programme.desc: The first pump action shotgun - a Winchester Model 1897; a hunter's collection of prize big game, full-body taxidermy.

programme.title: Auction Hunters: Don't Taze Me Bro
programme.desc: Two fierce opponents best Ton and Allen at the auction, leaving the duo in the lurch with a "trash unit" and little chance of breaking even for the day. And Ton gets tazed.

programme.title: Auction Hunters: Ton-Geons & Dragons
programme.desc: While Ton soups up their new truck, Allen heads to an auction solo and encounters Carolyn and CJ. There he discovers LARP weaponry, a sword collection, and falconry gear, before Ton reveals their new ride: Betty.

programme.title: Auction Hunters: Voodoo Moola
programme.desc: Allen & Ton hit a storage auction in Louisiana's Capitol City and quickly find themselves in trouble with the law! The guys uncover a pair of racing lawnmowers and then stumble on an antique barber chair from the early 1900s!

programme.title: Auction Hunters: Dead Aim
programme.desc: Ton & Allen face-off against an auction buyer with shady bidding tactics and even more questionable fashion sense. The guys find a custom-built monowheel & a rifle designed to ward off grave robbers in the early 19th century!

programme.title: Auction Hunters: Choo-Choo, Cha-Ching!
programme.desc: Ton and Allen get a surprise second chance after nearly getting shut out in the small town of Palmer. They uncover a motorized railroad maintenance car from the 1960's and a pre-WWI Japanese semi-automatic pistol.

programme.title: Auction Hunters: Diesel Digs
programme.desc: The guys head to the industrial hub of LA to score some heavy machinery and unearth a 1981 Bobcat 743 Frontloader, one of the first powered by a diesel engine and a trench-defending WWI German Sturmtrupp helmet bashing mace.

programme.title: Auction Hunters: Big Sis Ducks Out
programme.desc: Big Sis drops some bad news as the guys clash with Carolyn over a carnival unit at an auction in Long Beach. Ton and Allen unearth a Native American tomahawk, a dunk tank and a rare gun that can fire three shots at once.

programme.title: Auction Hunters: Home on the Gun Range
programme.desc: Barely making it to an auction in Corona, CA, Allen and Ton buy an overstuffed unit and find an antique crossbow, a rare 10-gauge Winchester shotgun and a 1940s Kiss-o-Meter.

programme.title: Criminal Confessions

programme.title: Criminal Confessions

programme.title: Criminal Confessions

programme.title: Meet, Marry, Murder: Malcolm Webster
programme.desc: The women in Malcolm Webster’s life seemed prone to bad luck. One died in a car crash. Another suffered a mysterious illness. A third, a keen sailor, found her life jacket punctured. How long before police realised this was more than coincidence?

programme.title: Meet, Marry, Murder: Stephen Searle
programme.desc: Stephen and Anne Searle were the grandparents at the helm of a large, close-knit family. But Stephen was having an affair, and Anne had been pushed to drink by her husband’s controlling behaviour. Neither was destined to enjoy a peaceful retirement.

programme.title: Meet, Marry, Murder: Ann Marie Anastasi
programme.desc: Ann Marie and Anthony Anastasi had four children, a big house on a desirable street… and a younger lover living in their basement. It was a toxic love triangle, and one that was destined to end in tragedy.

programme.title: Meet, Marry, Murder: Hannegret Donnelly

programme.title: Meet, Marry, Murder: Iain Lawrence
programme.desc: Ian and Sally Lawrence were a glamorous couple, and they looked like they had it all. But their marriage suffered when Ian’s star started to fall as Sally’s career rocketed. And when the couple suffered a terrible car crash, only one came out alive.

programme.title: Meet, Marry, Murder: Chaz Higgs

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett: The Shroud of Turin
programme.desc: Is this famous piece of linen cloth the actual burial cloth of Jesus Christ or a clever Medieval hoax?

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett: Energy Healing
programme.desc: Several energy healers claim they are able to produce spontaneous miraculous healing through a laying on of hands. Claims of similar such healing by a man in a remote village in Brazil, known as John of God are also examined.

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett
programme.desc: What on Earth are they spraying? Richard travels into the field to meet several researchers who present compelling evidence that chemicals or biological agents are being deliberately sprayed into the atmosphere by some shadowy government agency.

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett
programme.desc: Richard meets members of the 9/11 Truth movement who challenge the official version of events concerning the attack on the U.S. Pentagon. Researchers present evidence to suggest the attack was perpetrated by some rogue element inside the U.S. government.

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett
programme.desc: Were the Mercury, Genesis, Apollo and Space Shuttle programs mere window dressing designed for public consumption?

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett
programme.desc: Richard speaks with the founders of Architects and Engineers for 911 Truth and Firefighters for 911 Truth who present their case that the WTC towers were brought down by controlled demolition on 9/11.

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett
programme.desc: A serious investigation of the long-held theory that Elvis Presley faked his death in August 1977. Jokes of Elvis sightings in Kalamazoo aside, there is a considerable volume of evidence to suggest Elvis may not have left the building

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett
programme.desc: A lawyer and from the Pacific Northwest claims to have been part of a secret government time travel experiment, known as Project Pegasus.

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett: GMO - Genetically Modified Food
programme.desc: Richard investigates claims crop plants created for human or animal consumption using the latest molecular biology techniques are unsafe.

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett: The Birthers - Obama's Birth Certificate
programme.desc: Richard interviews several prominent 'Birthers' who present evidence that proves Barack Obama is not eligible to be President because he does not meet the qualifications of a natural born citizen.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: In 1975, 15-year-old Martha Moxley is murdered in Belle Haven, Connecticut. The case goes cold until 2002, when Michael Skakel, a family member, is convicted based on new evidence. Skakel maintains his innocence.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: On December 7, 1993, Colin Ferguson killed six and injured nineteen in the Long Island Railroad Massacre. Defending himself at trial, he bizarrely questioned his own victims and was sentenced to over 300 years in prison.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: In 1995 Paris, Guy Georges, a serial killer, was linked to multiple murders through DNA and later confessed in court. He was sentenced to life in prison, leading to the establishment of a DNA database for offenders in France.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: In 1990, Danny Rolling killed five students in Gainesville, Florida. He confessed to the crimes, pleaded guilty to avoid the death penalty, but was ultimately sentenced to death and executed 16 years later.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: In a gruesome 1989 case, Tracey Wigginton and her accomplices lure Edward Baldock with promises of a good time, only to brutally stab him to death, sparking a sensational trial that raised questions about the roles of her accomplices.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: In July 2000, Sarah Payne is abducted and murdered. Roy Whiting, a convicted sex offender, is found guilty and sentenced to life in 2001. Sarah’s case leads to the implementation of “Sarah’s Law” in the UK in 2011.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: Nathaniel Bar-Jonah, a paedophile, is convicted in 2002 for multiple crimes but not for the 1996 disappearance of Zachary Ramsay. Zachary’s body is never found, and Bar-Jonah dies in prison in 2008.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: In 2001, British traveler Peter Falconio is presumed murdered in the Australian outback. Joanne Lees survives the attack, and Bradley John Murdoch is convicted of the murder in 2005. Peter's body is never found.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: In 2007, Meredith Kercher is murdered in Italy. Amanda Knox and Raffaele Sollecito are initially convicted but later exonerated. Rudy Guede is convicted. The Kercher family still seeks justice.

programme.title: Bang Goes The Theory
programme.desc: The origins of weather; seasons allow life to form; Jem checks out wave power in Scotland.

programme.title: Bang Goes The Theory

programme.title: Hitler's Propaganda Machine: Power and Persuasion

programme.title: Yu-Gi-Oh! VRAINS: Standing Tall
programme.desc: The Tower of Hanoi is nearly complete – leaving Varis a win away from deleting LINK VRAINS once and for all!

programme.title: Transformers: Armada: Gale
programme.desc: A new minicon activates inside a highway tunnel, and the race is on to see who reach it first. Hot Shot is becoming more proficient with the Star Sabre. A new transformer arrives, claiming to be an Autobot, but Rad has his doubts.

programme.title: Yu-Gi-Oh! VRAINS: Kolter’s Khronicles
programme.desc: The Hanoi have expanded their reach beyond the boundaries of LINK VRAINS, and Kolter’s chronicling the chaos!

programme.title: MetaJets
programme.desc: Meta-Squadron has managed to capture Fly-Girl and Crusher, forcing Raven to offer a trade - the release of his agents for the return of a missing Metajets officer

programme.title: Massive Monster Mayhem
programme.desc: Master Mayhem has created a new chart-topping pop trio: The Doo Doo Dooms! These three musical monsters will melt your heart, then melt your planet! Meanwhile Eye Eye Eye will do anything for some peace and quiet… like conquer Earth!

programme.title: Street Sharks
programme.desc: The sharks accidentally get sent to the future where Piranoid has spread an air-born gene-slamming virus which has turned most of the world into subservient Seaviates. Only Bends' great great great grandson and a few other rebels didn't catch the virus.

programme.title: Yu-Gi-Oh! VRAINS: Once Upon A Time
programme.desc: As Blue Angel's biggest fan, Spectre knows all her dueling tricks and techniques - which makes him the fiercest foe she's ever faced!

programme.title: Magi-Nation
programme.desc: Korg's theft of a scroll from Naroom's library sends our heroes in pursuit of the Shadow Magi to the forbidding desert realm of the Sands of D’Resh. They ride aboard a Quanjuel – a horse-like creature that can travel through magical vortexes.

programme.title: Street Sharks
programme.desc: In PART TWO, Paradigm offers to help the government scientists with the Street Sharks.

programme.title: Magi-Nation
programme.desc: A battle between Gorgram, Korg & Zed and the Final Dreamers result in Gorgram being sent to the Dream Plane! But just when they all think they're rid of him forever, a clue from the Book of Elders hints that a Dream Plane Hyren exists…

programme.title: Camp Lakebottom: High Plains Garbage Eater / Dream a Little Scream
programme.desc: The gang finds themselves in the middle of a good ol' fashioned show down!

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Movement - Separation
programme.desc: When some children in the park invite Barney to join them for a race, BJ and Baby Bop decide he'll need their help. They show him several ways to get ready for the big race by warming up and stretching.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: Three kids try to build a pretend farm with disastrous results because they won't work together. Barney and his friends try to cheer up Olivia.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: As he watches birds fly overhead, Riff begins to daydream of flying himself. After seeing all the Moms playing with their kids in the Park, Baby Bop wonders what makes somebody a Mommy.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Venice, Anyone?
programme.desc: Emma can't play with her friends because she has to complete a school project about her family. Before you know it, Barney brings Emma on a trip to Venice to learn more about her great granddad.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: BJ and Riff find a pirate map, but in order to find the treasure, they have to be able to count. It’s time for a music concert in the park.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: Baby Bop can't find anybody to play with her. When the others see her feeling sad, they decide to play whatever she wants to play. The dinos and kids are planning a camp-out at the park.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: A creepy spider inspires Barney to tell the story of “The Reluctant Dragon.”

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: Baby Bop is surprised by the first day of cold weather, Barney helps her see that winter is a wonderful time. On a hot summer day, Barney teaches BJ about all the activities you can do in the summer.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): White Belt, Black Heart
programme.desc: Splinter's ancient sensei, Master Mogo-San, arrives from Japan, seeking to find his grandson, to hand him a precious family heirloom - a sacred white ninja belt.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Night of the Rogues
programme.desc: Determined to stop the turtles once and for all, Shredder enlists some of the most memorable villains from past episodes into an army of 'rogues'!

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Attack of the Neutrinos
programme.desc: When Krang captures the Neutrino's, he subjects the fun-loving, hot-rodding teenagers from Dimension X to a brain treatment which turns them evil!

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Escape from the Planet of the Turtleoids
programme.desc: The Turtles must once again help their Turtleoid friend Kurma defend the planet Shellri-La from alien invaders.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Revenge of the Fly
programme.desc: When Baxter Stockman, AKA the Fly, gets his pincers on some of Krang and Shredder's mutagen, he uses it to start turning everyone in the city into giant hideous mutant insects!

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Atlantis Awakes
programme.desc: The Turtles join forces with Merdude, a strange fish-creature, and journey with him to the fabulous underwater city of Atlantis.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Dirk Savage: Mutant Hunter
programme.desc: When eccentric zillionaire A.J. Howard hires Dirk Savage, a professional mercenary, to rid the city of its 'mutant problem', the first mutants he goes after are the Turtles!

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Invasion of the Krangazoids
programme.desc: It's trouble for the Turtles when Krang makes five clones of himself and sends them up to do his bidding in the city.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Combat Land
programme.desc: The Turtles are invited to be the first guests at combat Land, an amusement park where visitors fight a wide variety of robotic warriors from various historical eras.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Shredder Triumphant!
programme.desc: The Turtles face their greatest challenge ever when they become trapped in Dimension X--while Shredder and Krang bring the Technodrome out of the ocean depths and at last conquer the city!
